script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for SOXX. Wednesday — here is your SOXX recap. The portfolio closed up zero point one three percent on the session. A fractional gain, but the headlines underneath it were anything but quiet.

Advanced Micro Devices just signed a multi-billion-dollar deal with Anthropic. Gigawatt-scale deployment starting in twenty twenty-seven. That is not a pilot program — that is a structural commitment from one of the most-funded AI labs in the world, and it lands directly in the middle of Nvidia's territory.

No macro news was available for Wednesday's session. Markets navigated a mixed tape, with oil prices and Treasury yields applying pressure across risk assets. The Nasdaq finished in positive territory, lifted by strong capital expenditure signals from major technology earnings after the close, offering some cushion to the broader semiconductor complex.

The Advanced Micro Devices and Anthropic deal is the lead story. Multiple reports confirmed late in Wednesday's session that the two companies have agreed to a multi-billion-dollar partnership covering gigawatt-scale AI infrastructure, with deployment beginning in twenty twenty-seven. Anthropic, the maker of Claude, is committing to Advanced Micro Devices silicon at a scale that signals genuine competitive pressure on the dominant player in AI accelerators. The deal comes as hyperscalers have been diversifying their chip supply chains aggressively. Advanced Micro Devices also got a tailwind from strong capital expenditure guidance out of Alphabet after the close, suggesting the AI buildout spending cycle is not decelerating. For a position that sits at eight point three nine percent of this portfolio, that combination of a landmark customer win and favorable macro capex signals is a meaningful development to watch into Thursday's session.

Advanced Micro Devices was up one point four five percent on the session. That move came before full confirmation of the Anthropic deal hit wires — meaning the market may not have fully priced the news yet. The catalyst to watch is any additional commentary on deal scope or competing responses from rivals. Moving to Micron Technology, down one point one seven percent on Wednesday. The picture here is genuinely split. Fortune reported Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung have each crossed one trillion dollars in market capitalization this year on surging memory demand, a framing tied to the so-called memory supercycle. At the same time, a Seeking Alpha analyst published a strong sell rating, citing weak demand signals, falling artificial intelligence token costs, and significant earnings-per-share downside risk — flagging as much as seventy-six percent downside. That is a wide analytical gap, and the divergence is worth monitoring closely. Turning to Nvidia, up two point three zero percent Wednesday. Nvidia joined the Prometheus project, a Department of Energy initiative led by Idaho National Laboratory alongside Amazon Web Services, targeting artificial intelligence-accelerated nuclear deployment. The three-year partnership carries sixty million dollars in federal funding. A clean energy adjacency is a new narrative thread for Nvidia's industrial positioning. Over at Broadcom, up two point six seven percent — the strongest gain among the primary holdings Wednesday. No company-specific news drove the move, but the AI infrastructure capex signals from earnings season appear to have lifted the stock broadly, consistent with Broadcom's exposure to custom silicon and networking for hyperscalers. Meanwhile, Intel was down two point six eight percent, the sharpest loss in the portfolio Wednesday. No specific news catalyst was identified, but Intel continues to trade with elevated sensitivity to any macro pressure on the semiconductor complex, and the stock remains under structural scrutiny heading into its next earnings cycle. Applied Materials fell one point eight eight percent. The equipment sector broadly faced pressure Wednesday alongside rising yields, and Applied Materials, given its exposure to capital spending cycles from chipmakers, is particularly sensitive to that dynamic. Watch for any commentary out of memory chip customers given the mixed signals on demand. KLA Corporation was off one point three two percent. No specific news on Wednesday, but as a wafer inspection and process control equipment name, KLA tends to track applied materials and the broader equipment group closely. No divergence of note here. Marvell Technology was up one point four six percent on the session. The name benefited from the same AI infrastructure tailwinds supporting the broader group, with custom silicon for data centers remaining a core growth driver. The Anthropic and Advanced Micro Devices news is indirectly positive for the custom chip ecosystem that Marvell competes within. Lam Research fell zero point eight four percent Wednesday. Another equipment name under modest pressure in line with sector dynamics. Upcoming earnings from memory chip manufacturers will be the primary driver of sentiment for Lam Research in the near term. And Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing was off zero point eight zero percent. The company sits at the center of every meaningful chip narrative in this portfolio — the Anthropic deal, the Nvidia nuclear project, Broadcom's custom silicon — all flow through its fabrication capacity eventually. Geopolitical headline risk remains the variable to monitor.

Three data points to watch in the next forty-eight hours. First, any formal earnings release or guidance from major cloud providers following Wednesday's after-hours reports, which will clarify artificial intelligence capital expenditure trajectories directly relevant to this portfolio. Second, Micron Technology analyst commentary responding to the competing bull and bear cases published Wednesday — the divergence is unusually wide. Third, any official announcement expanding the scope of the Advanced Micro Devices and Anthropic agreement, particularly around hardware specifics and volume commitments.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for SOXX. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch for whether the Anthropic deal confirmation moves Advanced Micro Devices at the open — that will set the tone for the entire portfolio early Thursday.
